aws-emr:variant-type:v3.notes: EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 introduces native VARIANT data type support on Iceberg V3 tables; the engine-side capability applies regardless of catalog. (Per AWS EMR 8.0 GA announcement, May 27, 2026.)
caveats: S3 Tables-side documentation does not yet explicitly document VARIANT type support

aws-emr:shredded-variant:v3.notes: EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 supports shredded VARIANT using the standard Apache Parquet shredded implementation; the engine-side capability applies regardless of catalog.
caveats: S3 Tables-side documentation does not yet explicitly document shredded VARIANT support

aws-emr:geometry-type:v3.notes: Geometry type support with S3 Tables not explicitly documented

aws-emr:nanosecond-timestamps:v3.notes: Nanosecond timestamp support with S3 Tables not explicitly documented

aws-emr:lineage:v3.notes: EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 supports Iceberg V3 row lineage with `_row_id` and `_last_updated_sequence_number` metadata fields for row-level change tracking; the engine-side capability applies regardless of catalog.
caveats: S3 Tables-side documentation does not yet explicitly document lineage support

aws-emr:snowflake-horizon-catalog:v2.notes: EMR Spark natively supports the Iceberg REST catalog spec via iceberg-spark-runtime. Configure with spark.sql.catalog.<name>.type=rest pointing to the Horizon Catalog endpoint. Direct REST catalog connections support both reads and writes. Note: when routing through AWS Glue Catalog Federation, writes are not supported — that is a Glue/EMR federation path limitation, not an OSS Spark or Horizon Catalog limitation.
caveats: Write via direct REST catalog API is supported
Write via Glue Catalog Federation path is not supported (Glue engine limitation)

aws-emr:snowflake-horizon-catalog:v3.notes: V3 support via EMR Spark requires iceberg-spark-runtime 1.7.0+.
caveats: Requires iceberg-spark-runtime 1.7.0+ for V3 table support

aws-emr:deletion-vectors:v2.notes: Deletion vectors are a V3 feature; not applicable to Iceberg V2

aws-emr:deletion-vectors:v3.notes: EMR 8.0 / Spark 4.0.2 supports Iceberg V3 deletion vectors (Puffin files), replacing positional delete files for efficient merge-on-read row-level deletes.

links.label: Iceberg v3 deletion vectors on Amazon EMR
